page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 62 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled (2 threads per core) at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate matrix mathematics and vector processing workloads. Additionally, the instance features a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-8-lssd demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance (2299.39) and Geekbench single-core score (1899.0). It achieves top-tier performance (top 10%) in single-threaded gzip compression (35.4 GB/sec) and Geekbench text processing (2924.0). Multi-core operations, such as Geekbench multi-core (7155.0) and stress-ng multi-core (9339.42), align with average tier expectations. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory subsystem testing; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 691.79 GB/sec, the PassMark memory score falls into the weak category (bottom 25% at 1720.0), indicating potential bottlenecks under specific memory-intensive access patterns.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-8-lssd offers a high-density memory profile paired with dedicated compute resources and integrated high-speed NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage attachments for temporary data. This balance makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that require rapid local scratch space. Appropriate workloads include medium-scale database operations, text processing pipelines, single-threaded compression tasks, and lightweight language model inference, where its strong single-core performance and AMX-driven matrix capabilities can be leveraged effectively.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8-lssd is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 62 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor (x86_64 architecture) with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it operates at 2.3 GHz and includes a 504 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, with top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing, alongside average multi-core and database execution. However, PassMark memory benchmarks indicate a weak performance tier for certain memory operations. This configuration provides a cost-effective balance of high memory density and fast local storage without the overhead of external disks. It is well-suited for database operations, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8-lssd is an 8-vCPU, 62 GB RAM memory-optimized instance with an Intel Xeon CPU and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8-lssd is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, advanced matrix extensions, and high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 8 vCPUs and 62 GB RAM
Includes a high-speed 402 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Delivers top-tier single-threaded compression and text processing performance
Suited for database operations, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference
